title: What does it mean to have a partner?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Colin Wong and Abigail Brown organized a last-minute microwedding at Dearly Studios due to a January 2026 blizzard.
FX's 'Love Story' prompts nostalgia for 1990s communication, highlighting dinner conversations without phones.
Debate arises over the use of 'partner' versus 'boyfriend,' questioning if young couples use the term too freely.

title: After loss and cancer, a Burlington runner keeps moving toward Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Adam Averback, 44, from Burlington, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team MGH.
Averback started running after losing his father to glioblastoma and surviving testicular cancer himself.
He overcame a serious blood infection during training and aims to honor his father's memory and survivorship.

title: New Patriots S Kevin Byard working out with rumored New England trade target A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots have shown strong interest in acquiring WR A.J. Brown from the Eagles, but an offer was declined.
Eagles may wait until June 1 to trade A.J. Brown to save salary cap space, seeking a first-round pick.
New Patriots S Kevin Byard and Brown, former Titans teammates, have been working out together, fueling trade rumors.

title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens ended his sophomore season at Boston College with 47 points over 34 games.
Bruins GM Don Sweeney is considering signing Hagens to an entry-level contract after BC's season ended.
Hagens, a top prospect, is projected as a future top-six player after being drafted seventh in 2025.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Bradley Cooper directs Will Arnett in comedy-drama 'Is This Thing On?' streaming on Disney+.
Chuck Norris stars in 'Lone Wolf McQuade,' streaming on Tubi, Pluto TV, Plex, Prime Video, and YouTube.
'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man,' starring Cillian Murphy, available on Netflix.

title: ‘We’ll see’: Alex Cora mum on future with Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora's contract with the Boston Red Sox runs through the 2027 season, and he remains uncertain about future extensions.
Cora, who led the Red Sox to a World Series win in 2018, has experienced mixed seasons, including back-to-back losing years.
Cora is 134 wins away from surpassing Terry Francona for second-most managerial wins in Red Sox history.

title: The Bruins’ second line isn’t just a pleasant surprise. They’ve been one of the NHL’s best this year.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins' second line of Viktor Arvidsson, Pavel Zacha, and Casey Mittelstadt has outscored opponents 35-16 this season.
This line has achieved a +19 goal differential, ranking second in the NHL behind only Colorado's top line.
Zacha leads with 23 goals and 51 points in 66 games, while Arvidsson and Mittelstadt have also excelled.

title: He’s running Boston to support kids and families at MGH
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Andrew Mahr, 37, from Needham, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support MGH Pediatric Cancer Center.
Mahr first ran Boston in 2014 after witnessing young patients at Heartbreak Hill and the aftermath of the 2013 bombing.
He emphasizes raising funds for pediatric cancer research, which receives less funding compared to adult cancer research.

title: ‘More money in my pocket is a no-brainer’: Readers weigh in on proposed income tax cut
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A proposed Massachusetts income tax cut could reduce the rate from 5% to 4%, cutting state revenue by $5.1 billion annually.
A Boston.com poll shows 78% of respondents support the tax cut, which would save the median household about $1,250 annually.
Gov. Maura Healey opposes the tax cut, citing potential harm to state finances and economic competitiveness, contrasting public support for it.

title: A voodoo doll, a love triangle, and an alleged poisoning: Testimony begins in Salisbury antifreeze murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judy Church is on trial in Salisbury for allegedly murdering her boyfriend Leroy Fowler with antifreeze in 2022.
Prosecutors say Church's 'jokes' about poisoning Fowler reveal her motive in the context of a love triangle.
Defense argues Church's actions were misunderstood and that she had no motive to kill as Fowler ended his affair.
